notes.note: Servius Sulpicius Galba, one of Caesar's assassins and an ancestor of the emperor Galba; his eyewitness dispatch is the fullest surviving account of the battle of Forum Gallorum (14 April 43 BC).

notes.anchor: a portion of the re-enrolled veterans
notes.note: Renders evocati: time-served legionaries recalled to the standards individually, who served as a seasoned elite outside the regular legionary structure.

notes.anchor: the Martian Legion (which I used to command)
notes.note: The Legio Martia, one of two legions that deserted Antony for the Republican side in late 44 BC; it formed the shock element of the consular army and bore the brunt of the fighting at Forum Gallorum.
The Via Aemilia, the great trunk road of Cisalpine Gaul running from Ariminum through Forum Gallorum to Placentia; the fighting was strung out along its line east of Mutina.

notes.anchor: at the fourth hour of the night
notes.note: Roman timekeeping divided night and day each into twelve hours; the fourth hour of the night falls roughly four hours after sunset.

notes.anchor: Two eagles, sixty standards, of Antony's have been brought in
notes.note: The aquila (eagle) was the sacred standard of a whole legion; its capture, with sixty lesser unit standards (signa), measures the scale of Antony's defeat.
